author: Nand Lal
title: Sher-i-Babar
gloss: The picture of the lion, with a brief account of its form, habits, etc.
publisher: Nand Lal and Co., Co-operative Works
notes: Lion nature-study wall-chart, continuing the Nand Lal & Co. picture-sheet series from p35. '(Designed for educational purposes.)'.

author: Naqi Muhammad
title: Zinat-ul-Haram
gloss: Or book containing lectures on punctuality in performing daily prayers, superstitions, sanitation, female education, &c., intended for Muhammadan girls.
notes: 'Adornment of the Home' — a conduct-book for Muslim girls (prayer punctuality, anti-superstition, sanitation, female education), 2000 copies; the domestic-reform/girls'-improvement genre bundling piety with modern hygiene and schooling.

title: Pran Data
gloss: An advertisement of a medicine named Pran Data or the strength giver prepared by Jai Dayal, Vaid.
copyright: Pandit Jai Dayal of Lahore; No. 13, dated the 28th January, 1910.
notes: A free advertising booklet for a patent tonic ('Pran Data, the strength giver') by an Ayurvedic vaid — commercial patent-medicine puffery in the print record (cf. the Ayurvedic pharmacy price-list p32 s9); the strength/vigour-tonic market. This is the entry Q4 forward-referenced.

title: Punjab Sindh Chhetr
gloss: The Sixth Annual Report of the working of the Punjab Sindh Chhetr, or asylum for Hindu fakirs of Rikhi Kesh and Kankhal.
publisher: Bhagwan Das, Sethi, Manager, of the Asylum
notes: Annual report of a Hindu sadhu-asylum (Punjab Sindh Kshetra) at Rishikesh/Kankhal — institutional/charitable-organization reporting; the Hindu-ascetic welfare network of the upper Ganges pilgrimage towns. Dual date: 1st Har, Sammat 1965.

author: Raj Narayan
title: Rajputon ki Zindgi
gloss: A booklet giving a short account of the mode of living of the Rajputs, their customs, habits and social observances.
notes: 'Life of the Rajputs' — Raj Narayan (the Rajput-biography author of p25) extends his Rajput project into ethnographic/customary description; the Rajput-identity codification via the Dipak Rajput Press. Self-ethnography of a caste-community.

author: Raj Narayan
title: Rasm-o-Riwaj
gloss: A pamphlet giving an account of the peculiar beliefs, ceremonies, customs, superstitions, etc., of the peoples of the various parts of the world.
notes: Author dash-continuation of s39. A comparative-ethnography of world customs — the popular-anthropology/'customs of nations' genre; the same author moving from Rajput self-description to a survey of world peoples (the ethnographic gaze turned outward).

author: Ram Nath
title: Jadu ki Pahli
gloss: A booklet on magic and sleight of hand.
notes: Conjuring/sleight-of-hand manual, an astonishing 11th edition — a durable popular-entertainment bestseller (the highest edition-number seen in Q1); the stage-magic/recreational-print market.
